ground_truth.title: In The News - High-Impact Tutoring - Reading Corps, Math Corps
ground_truth.main_content:
We’re changing the world, one student at a time.

Our Programs in the News

High-impact tutoring program seeks to improve Brown County reading proficiency rates
At the local level, 115 students within Brown County benefited from Reading Corps services over the past year. These 115 local students received 6,312 tutoring sessions, accounting to over 119,000 minutes of invaluable literacy support. – Go Press Times

Reading Corps brings high-impact tutoring to schools across Wisconsin
The program uses one-on-one or two-on-one tutors to teach children in kindergarten through third grade to read, using the now-preferred approach known as “science of reading.” – Wisconsin Public Radio

Many Wisconsin students struggle with reading. How does one tutoring group help schools?
Only 33% of Wisconsin fourth-graders are at or above proficient reading levels. That’s where Reading Corps comes in. Kindergarten and first-grade students who worked with the program saw a year’s worth of improvement in a semester. – Green Bay Press Gazette

This Nonprofit Runs the Nation’s 3 Largest Tutoring Programs. Here’s What It’s Learned
Tutoring is having quite the moment, for pretty obvious reasons. What’s less clear is whether “high-dosage tutoring” will turn out to be another overhyped fad or something that actually makes a difference. – Rick Hess, Education Week

Florida Early Learning Corps Receives $1M Investment
“I am really grateful for the opportunity to champion early childhood education in the Legislature and to make sure that we are empowering the best practices that we know exist,” said Senator Calatayud, who carried the proposal throughout the last legislative session. – The Miami Times

AmeriCorps delivers large grant to Poughkeepsie
AmeriCorps State and National Director Sonali Nijhawan was in Poughkeepsie on Thursday to present a $240,000 American Rescue Plan grant to expand AmeriCorps programs in the City of Poughkeepsie and Dutchess County in partnership with local institutions. – Mid Hudson News

World Tutor Day is this week
With a reminder of the importance of tutors, Sadie O’Connor, president of Reading Corps and Math Corps talks about the need for tutors in Minnesota and how you can help. – CBS News

On the importance of getting kids reading for fun
Tips for getting your kids into books from the director of Reading Corps. – CBS News

Lawmakers get a tour of a newer tutoring program in Idaho
While it hasn’t been around long, it’s already making a difference in the classroom. – KMVT

AmeriCorps looking for tutors in the Hattiesburg area
AmeriCorps is looking for tutors to assist students in the Hattiesburg Public School System with developing their reading and math skills – WDAM

TEACHER VOICE: Through tutoring, I discovered I love teaching middle schoolers
Tutoring can jump-start careers for college grads and help address the post-pandemic teacher shortage – The Hechinger Report

Reading tutors help prepare early learners for kindergarten
Shevrin Jones, executive director of Florida’s Reading Corps, says reading is the one foundation for children that can’t be overlooked – K-12 Dive

It’s time to declare an education emergency
We must act to reverse a disaster. – StarTribune
